title: Rocket Lab (RKLB): Q1 2026 Earnings Review
text: Rocket Lab released exceptional Q1 2026 results, featuring record revenue of $200.3 million beating guidance, a $2.2 billion backlog up 108% year-over-year, and multiple strategic wins. The stock surged 34% to break $100 per share for the first time. Key financial metrics include revenue of $200.3 million, up 63.5% year-over-year and up 11.5% quarter-over-quarter, GAAP gross margin of 38.2% above 34-36% guidance, adjusted EBITDA of negative $11.8 million beating negative $21-27 million guidance, cash position of $1.48 billion plus $2 billion plus total liquidity access, and backlog of approximately $2.2 billion with 41.5% from launch and 58.5% from space systems. Launch Services signed 31 new Electron and HASTE contracts in Q1 alone, exceeding all of 2025's total. Launch backlog now exceeds 70 missions. The company secured a $190 million MACH-TB 2.0 order from Department of Defense, positioning HASTE as category leader. For Neutron development, customers signed multi-launch contracts despite the vehicle not yet flying, demonstrating confidence in medium-lift demand. Q4 2026 remains the first-flight target. Space Systems revenue reached $136.7 million, up 57.2% year-over-year. Rocket Lab acquired Motiv Space Systems adding Mars robotics expertise and completed Mynaric acquisition adding optical communications. The company unveiled Gauss electric propulsion thruster with 200 plus units produced.
